careerbuilder.notes: CareerBuilder is part of the Monster network. Job postings on Monster+ plans are distributed to CareerBuilder. Standalone pricing requires direct contact with sales.

cpc_range.description: Employers pay per job seeker click. Most common model for major job boards.

ppa_range.description: Pay per qualified application received. Growing model for specialized roles.

duration_based_range.description: Flat fee for time-based postings, common on niche boards.

subscription_range.description: Fixed monthly fee for unlimited or tiered postings.

technology.notes: Tech apply rates surged to 7.14% in 2025 due to white-collar recession. CPC lower due to higher applicant volume, but cost per hire remains elevated due to longer screening cycles.

healthcare.notes: Healthcare consistently has the highest recruitment costs. Nursing and specialized roles can exceed $10,000 per hire. Persistent labor shortages drive up CPC.

retail.notes: Retail has lowest cost per hire due to high volume and lower skill requirements. Seasonal hiring patterns cause CPC fluctuations.

finance.notes: Finance and banking roles have moderate CPC but higher CPA due to compliance and credential screening requirements.

manufacturing.notes: Manufacturing CPC is moderate but rising. Skilled trades positions command higher CPC than general labor roles.

hospitality.notes: Similar to retail -- high volume, lower cost per hire, but high turnover drives total recruitment spend up.

engineering.notes: Engineering roles (civil, mechanical, electrical) have similar costs to tech but lower apply rates.

cybersecurity.notes: Cybersecurity is among the most expensive verticals to hire for. Talent scarcity drives high CPC and CPA.

data_science.notes: Data science and AI/ML roles command premium pricing. High demand with limited qualified talent pool.

entry_level.notes: Lower CPC due to larger applicant pools

senior.notes: Significantly higher CPC as talent pool narrows

executive.notes: Executive search often involves retained firms, not just job board advertising

small.notes: Higher per-hire cost due to less efficient processes, fewer economies of scale

medium.notes: Moderate costs with some process optimization

large.notes: Lower per-hire cost through volume discounts, established employer brand, ATS efficiency

trends.title: Cost-per-hire and CPA rose sharply in 2025
trends.summary: Despite a softer labor market in 2025, both cost-per-application and cost-per-hire increased significantly, driven by shifts in job board pricing models and programmatic media.
trends.source: Appcast 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report / HR Dive

trends.title: Apply rates improved but did not reduce CPA
trends.summary: Apply rates climbed to 5.19% in 2025, but rising CPC negated the improvement. More applications per candidate, but each click costs more.

trends.title: White-collar recession distorted recruitment funnel
trends.summary: Tech apply rates surged to 7.14% while healthcare struggled at lower rates. The bifurcation created vastly different CPC/CPA dynamics across industries.

trends.title: Shift to pay-per-application models increasing costs
trends.summary: Major job boards are shifting from CPC to pay-per-application and performance-based models, causing increased costs in recruitment advertising especially for healthcare and frontline roles.

trends.title: Cost per hire climbed to $1,340 in recruitment marketing
trends.summary: The 2026 Recruitment Marketing Benchmark Report shows cost per hire in recruitment marketing specifically (not total cost) reached $1,340.

trends.title: Average US cost per hire now $4,700-$4,800
trends.summary: Multiple sources (SHRM, Engagedly) confirm the average cost per hire in the US is between $4,700 and $4,800 as of early 2026, up from $4,000-$4,400 in prior years.

trends.title: Healthcare recruitment costs reaching $30,000 per hire
trends.summary: Healthcare and frontline roles now seeing cost-per-hire figures reaching $30,000 for specialized positions like nursing, driven by persistent labor shortages.

trends.title: Job board CPC range stabilizing at $0.50-$5.00
trends.summary: Across major platforms, CPC has stabilized in the $0.50-$5.00 range, with Indeed at the lower end and LinkedIn/ZipRecruiter at the higher end. Niche boards can exceed $5.

hidden_costs_of_hiring: Recruitment agency fees (15-25% of first year salary)
Interview time (estimated $500-$1,000 per hire in staff time)
Lost productivity during vacancy (estimated 1-3x daily salary per day vacant)
